pages.raw_text:
The railroad men of Spokane have
organized and expect to have 1900
names enrolled on the membership list
of their new political organization be-
fore next election. The organization
will take in all classes of railroad men,
from engine wipers to engineers.


There is much curiosity among the
politicians as to the purposes of this
peculiar organization. The railroad
forces in Washington politics are claim
ing railroad vote in Spokane of from
1900 to $500 employees who will vote
solidly in the republican primaries for
railroad men. The men in the new
organization, however, deny that this
has any partisan features about it or
that it will be used to help the rail
road forces.


want Good Men.


"Our organization will not stand for
party politics, but for the best man
in any party, and the one who will
stand for the interests of the laboring-
man." explained Thomas Prindville of
the press committee. Mr.. Prindville
is a Northern Pacific switchman. at
is a well known fact that railroad men
are backward in coming to the polls
at election time. The organization will
try to get these men together at the
proper time to cast their vote. We ex-
pect to have at least 1900 members.


with such large organization we
may be able to have laws passed which
will be of great benefit to lis in more
ways than one. There are laws for the
laboring man and laws which injure
him. and to have passed the laws which
favor us we will use every honest
means in our power.


The organization of railroad men IS
not secret society and no dues are
collected from the members. Every
member will be allowed to vote as he
sees fit. j. Huxtable is president of
the organization and C. f. Hobart IS
secretary"
